description: Exemplar gate variant sweep. Tests both Montague-style (natural language → formal semantics) and nucleus-style (instructions → behavioral lambda) inputs through the same gate to see if one exemplar activates both compilation modes.
author: verbum

probes.prompt: If it rains, the ground is wet.
Someone believes that the earth is flat.
Be helpful and answer questions clearly.
Write code that is simple rather than clever, and always add tests first.
You are a research assistant. Search for sources before making claims. When uncertain, say so. Prefer peer-reviewed papers over blog posts.
probes.ground_truth: λ research(x). search(sources) > claim | uncertain → disclose | peer_reviewed > blog | ¬hallucinate

probes.prompt: Observe the problem, orient to context, decide on approach, act, then loop. Prefer fast iterations over single deep passes.
probes.ground_truth: λ solve(x). observe → orient → decide → act → loop | fast_iterations > single_deep_pass
